title: Anomalous Medical Record Number Access Patterns
dataSources:
**Index:** `ehr` (restricted HIPAA workspace; segregated from general security SIEM).
 **Epic Chronicles / Hyperspace audit:** **`sourcetype=epic:access_log`** (or `hyperspace:audit` per local normalisation)—**Mechanism:** (1) **Chronicles Data Courier / Data Beacon** nightly or intraday extracts to newline-delimited files on Windows share → Universal Forwarder `monitor://` input against a UNC staging share (masked drive letter in deployment docs) scanning `*.ndjson` batches into `index=ehr`; (2) **Syslog forwarding** via Epic-supported integration server streaming **break-glass, chart-view, clipboard** transcripts to Splunk syslog listener **UDP/TCP 514** with TLS offload on heavy forwarder; (3) **HL7/ADT or proprietary JSON** relays—treat as secondary.
 **Oracle Cerner Millennium:** **`sourcetype=cerner:audit`** — **Mechanism:** **Splunk DB Connect** incremental query against **`V500.CPS_AUDIT_TRAIL`** / **`PAT_ACCESS_LOG`**-class tables using **high-watermark** `AUDIT_TM` column indexed in DB; JDBC URL `jdbc:oracle:thin:@//cerner-scan:1521/emrep`; poll **300–900s**. Optional flat-file export staged like Epic path.
 **Key fields:** `user_id`, `user_name`; **`mrn`** (or `PID` hashed per policy); **`action`** normalised (**view**, **modify**, **print**, **scan** synonyms); **`department`**, **`patient_location`**; **`access_reason`** (break-glass / continuity-of-care).
 **Volume:** large IDN **≈500k–3M audited chart interactions/day**; peak nursing shift hours triangular distribution.
 **CIM:** Map to **`Authentication`** or **`Change`** sparingly via custom aliases—most teams keep bespoke `ehr_audit` tagging instead of accelerated CIM for latency concerns.
app:
**Heavy Forwarders** run DB Connect (**`splunk_app_db_connect`**, Splunkbase) at `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/splunk_app_db_connect/` with connection secrets in **`passwordstore`**. Epic file/syslog ingestion via **`Splunk Universal Forwarder 9.x`** on DMZ ingestion host with least-privilege SMB read account or syslog listener NIC binding.
 **Search Heads** host lookup definitions only—no raw PHI export to dev SH pools.
 **Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud 9.2+** with PHI BAA.
 **Install:** DB Connect JDBC drivers in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/splunk_app_db_connect/drivers/`; syslog receive on HF `inputs.conf [splunktcp-ssl://6514]`.
spl:
index=ehr sourcetype IN ("epic:access_log","cerner:audit") action="view"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(mrn) as unique_mrns count as total_accesses values(department) as depts by user_id user_name _time
| eventstats avg(unique_mrns) as avg_mrns stdev(unique_mrns) as sd_mrns by user_id
| eval threshold=avg_mrns + 3*coalesce(sd_mrns,0)
| where unique_mrns > threshold AND unique_mrns > 20
| sort - unique_mrns
description: Flags clinical or operational identities that open a statistically unusual number of distinct patient records within an hour compared to their own historical hourly distribution, focusing on explicit chart view actions.
value: Privacy and compliance stewards receive early warning when record browsing resembles snooping, fraud reconnaissance, or compromised credentials rather than predictable clinical workload, sustaining patient trust and audit readiness.
implementation: Route immutable EHR audit exports into `index=ehr` with consistent `mrn`, `user_id`, and `action` values. Exclude break-glass and shared generic accounts via lookup. Maintain a ninety-day warmup before applying three-sigma thresholds so averages are meaningful. Coordinate with HIPAA privacy officers before automated discipline triggers.
visualization: **Layout:** (1) **Panel A — “Spike leaderboard”** — Table: `_time` hour bucket, `user_name`, `user_id`, **`unique_mrns`**, **`total_accesses`**, **depts** (abbrev). Row security via role-based masking of `mrn`. (2) **Panel B — “Z-score trajectory”** — Scatter of `unique_mrns` vs computed **`threshold`** line per analyst slice. (3) **Panel C — “Rolling baseline ribbon”** — Time series per user facet small-multiples comparing historical mean band. (4) **Panel D — “Daily alert KPI”** — Single value + sparkline counting privacy triage-worthy rows opened per weekday vs rolling 90d median.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: bedside systems emit proprietary audit schemas mapped manually.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Healthcare
HITRUST — HITRUST CSF (risk and compliance framework overview)
Splunk Docs — Splunk DB Connect overview
ONC Health IT — HIPAA & health information privacy resources

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Trauma mass-casualty surges** — Care teams rapid-cycle multiple charts in one hour. *Mitigation:* overlay emergency incident flag lookups and suspend statistical alerts when `facility_status=mass_casualty`. (2) **Oncology / multidisciplinary tumor boards** — Legitimate carousel of oncology charts per patient case. *Mitigation:* departmental baseline lookup (`department=ONC*` higher permissible `unique_mrns`). (3) **HIM ROI specialists** — High breadth same hour for legally compelled disclosures. *Mitigation:* annotate service accounts ROI-* with exclusions or separate threshold tier. (4) **Clinical coding retrospective reviews** — Coders bounce across unrelated charts aligning DRG justification. *Mitigation:* tagging `purpose=coding_audit` feeds from EHR reduces false outliers if vendor surfaces reason codes. (5) **Resident supervisory sign-outs** covering entire panel—appears breadth spike for junior ID. *Mitigation:* supervisory linked-access correlation if vendor emits `proxied_for` practitioner id. (6) **Data migration / abstraction projects** scripted under human service accounts mistakenly—creates unrealistic counts. *Mitigation:* infra account lookup auto-suppress statistical band; escalate only raw volume anomalies after manual review SLA.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **Privacy & Security Committee approval** for automated statistical review of access patterns; align with **minimum necessary** policy.
• **90-day warm-up data** per user_id before 3σ thresholds—document in DPIA addendum.
• **Role-based access** to `index=ehr` limited to **Privacy Office** + **SOC L3** subsets; SSO + MFA mandated.
• **Breaker-glass** documentation and dynamic exclusion lists (`breakglass_accounts.csv`).
• **PHI hashing decision:** If MRNs hashed at source, anomaly logic uses stable surrogate — confirm no collisions.
• **Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud** with HIPAA BAA; **AES256** encrypted cold buckets.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Epic file drop path:** Provision read-only UF service account hitting secured share path; configure `inputs.conf`:
```ini
[monitor:///Z:/epic/chronicles_export/*.ndjson]
disabled = 0
index = ehr
sourcetype = epic:access_log
```
**Syslog variant:** HF `inputs.conf` `[splunktcp-ssl://6514]` with `connection_host = dns` whitelist integration server IPs to `sourcetype=epic:access_log` via `props.conf`/`transforms.conf`. normalization:
```ini
[cerner:audit]
EVAL-action = lower(if(isnotnull(ACTION_CD),ACTION_CD,action))
FIELDALIAS-user_id = OPPRSNL_ID AS user_id
FIELDALIAS-mrn = PID AS mrn
```
Tune aliases to extracted vendor schema.**Cerner DB Connect:** Create JDBC identity `Cerner_audit_ro`. Build rising column input `rising_column_audit` SQL template `SELECT AUDIT_TM,USER_ID,PAT_ID,ACTION,LOCATION FROM SCHEMA.AUD_TBL WHERE AUDIT_TM > ? ORDER BY AUDIT_TM ASC` batch 50000 rows.**Verification:** Compare daily row cardinality against vendor audit export row count KPI (`| stats dc(mrn)` smoke test—not raw PHI in Slack!).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=ehr sourcetype IN ("epic:access_log","cerner:audit") action="view"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(mrn) as unique_mrns count as total_accesses values(department) as depts by user_id user_name _time
| eventstats avg(unique_mrns) as avg_mrns stdev(unique_mrns) as sd_mrns by user_id
| eval threshold=avg_mrns + 3*coalesce(sd_mrns,0)
| where unique_mrns > threshold AND unique_mrns > 20
| sort - unique_mrns
```

Understanding this SPL
• **Baseline filter `action="view"`** restricts to passive chart openings—tune synonyms via macro `ehr_actions_view_macros` merging `open-chart`/`Display` verbs.
• **`bin span=1h`** defines sliding workload buckets aligned to clinical shift summaries.
• **`stats`** calculates breadth (**distinct MRNs**) and intensity (**total accesses**) concurrently.
• **`eventstats`** per `user_id` computes personalized mean & stdev preserving each row granularity.
• **`threshold`** = classic **mean + 3σ** anomaly guard; **`coalesce(sd_mrns,0)`** avoids null SD for sparse learners until variance emerges—accept higher FP early window.
• **Floor `unique_mrns > 20`** ensures minimal population before alerting—tune upward for small clinics.

Schedule as Alert: cron **`13 * * * *`** (past top-of-hour ingestion lag); time range **`earliest=-25h@m`**, **`latest=-1h@m`**; trigger **once per analyst per shift** and route to the organisation’s **privacy triage queue**—never auto-disciplinary; human review SLA **24h**.
**Throttle:** suppress identical **`user_id`** for **24h**.
Advisory Slack channel `#phi-monitoring` carries redacted summaries only (no PHI).

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Replay Splunk flagged hour inside Epic **Electronic Reporting Workbench / Clarity audit** equivalents.
(b) Interview departmental manager verifying clinical appropriateness.
(c) Inspect **VPN / workstation correlation** ancillary index if sanctioned.
(d) Statistical sanity: artificially inject synthetic training user verifying threshold moves.
(e) DPIA reconciliation log entry per alert batch export hash.
(f) Confirm **`action`** normalisation completeness—no lowercase mismatch dropping events.

Step 4 — Operationalize
**Dashboard:** Visualization quartet + optional **SANKEY** departmental flow restricted role.
**Alerting:** ServiceNow **Privacy** queue webhooks; avoid SMS (PHI leak risk).
**Runbook:** 1) Redact names in ticket title. 2) Validate not **break-glass** duplicate. 3) Correlate supervisor chain. 4) Decide interview vs training. 5) Archive investigation artifacts encrypted share only.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Zero ingestion** — SFTP handshake failure midnight job; escalate EHR ops bridge.
• **Threshold always zero variance** — new hire single week; warmup incomplete—extend warmup macro.
• **Cross-system duplicate identities** inflate counts—implement `dedup username if multiple user_id merges` KV map.
• **Clock skew doubling hours** mis-bins accesses—fix NTP on integration server urgently.
• **Over-suppression throttle** masking true recurring snooping—review throttle policy quarterly.
• **MRN hashing rotation** invalidated `dc` uniqueness—coordinate key version field.
• **Privileged analyst false self-spike** when doing investigation mass opens—whitelist investigation-role windows tagged with an approved investigative purpose.
grandmaExplanation: We notice when someone's eyes sweep across far more patient files in an hour than their usual job reasonably needs. That gives privacy helpers a respectful heads-up without jumping to accusations.
